name: code-review
description: Review pull requests and diffs for correctness, style, and maintainability issues. Use when the user asks for a code review, feedback on a diff, or a pre-merge quality check.

name: spreadsheet-analysis
description: Analyze CSV and Excel spreadsheets: summarize columns, build pivot tables, and spot anomalies. Use when the user shares tabular data and asks questions about totals, trends, or outliers.

name: calendar-booking
description: Schedule, reschedule, and cancel calendar events across attendees and time zones. Use when the user wants to find an open slot, book a meeting, or resolve a scheduling conflict.

name: image-editing
description: Resize, crop, convert, and retouch images, including background removal and format conversion. Use when the user asks to modify an existing image file rather than generate a new one.

name: k8s-debugging
description: Diagnose Kubernetes problems: CrashLoopBackOff, OOMKilled pods, failing readiness probes, and misbehaving deployments. Use when the user reports broken workloads in a cluster.

name: sql-query-builder
description: Write and optimize SQL queries against relational databases, including joins, window functions, and index advice. Use when the user needs a query written, explained, or made faster.

name: web-scraping
description: Extract structured data from web pages: product listings, tables, and article text, with pagination handling. Use when the user wants data pulled off a website into a usable format.

name: document-translation
description: Translate documents between languages while preserving formatting, tone, and technical terminology. Use when the user asks to translate prose, docs, or UI copy.

name: git-workflows
description: Untangle git problems: interactive rebases, cherry-picks, conflict resolution, and history rewrites. Use when the user is stuck mid-rebase, has diverging branches, or lost commits.

name: pdf-processing
description: Merge, split, compress, and extract text or tables from PDF documents. Use when the user needs content pulled out of a PDF or several PDFs combined into one.

name: email-triage
description: Prioritize and summarize an email inbox: surface messages needing replies, draft responses, and archive noise. Use when the user asks what in their inbox actually matters.

name: ci-pipeline-doctor
description: Diagnose failing CI builds: flaky tests, broken caching, misconfigured workflows, and dependency drift. Use when the user reports a red pipeline or a build that only fails in CI.

name: data-visualization
description: Turn datasets into charts: line, bar, scatter, and heatmaps with sensible axes and legends. Use when the user asks to visualize data or improve an existing chart.

name: docs-search
description: Search internal documentation, wikis, and runbooks to answer questions with cited sources. Use when the user asks where something is documented or wants a policy or procedure looked up.

name: regex-builder
description: Craft, explain, and debug regular expressions with test cases for edge inputs. Use when the user needs a pattern matched, validated, or a cryptic regex deciphered.

name: api-mocking
description: Stand up mock REST and GraphQL endpoints with realistic fake data for demos and tests. Use when the user needs an API simulated before or instead of the real backend.

name: log-analysis
description: Parse and summarize application and access logs: error clustering, traffic patterns, and suspicious activity. Use when the user shares log files and wants signal extracted from them.

name: resume-screening
description: Evaluate resumes against a job description: rank candidates, flag gaps, and summarize strengths. Use when the user is shortlisting applicants for a role.

name: meeting-notes
description: Turn meeting transcripts or recordings into structured notes with decisions, action items, and owners. Use when the user shares a meeting and wants the takeaways.

name: dependency-audit
description: Audit project dependencies for known vulnerabilities, license conflicts, and outdated packages. Use when the user asks whether their dependency tree is safe to ship.

name: i18n-localization
description: Externalize hardcoded strings into locale files and adapt layouts, dates, and plurals per language. Use when the user is preparing an app for multiple languages.

name: dockerfile-optimizer
description: Shrink and speed up container builds: layer ordering, multi-stage builds, and smaller base images. Use when the user complains about image size or slow Docker builds.

name: unit-test-generation
description: Generate unit test suites for existing code, covering edge cases, error paths, and mocking of dependencies. Use when the user asks for tests to be written for a module or function.

name: financial-modeling
description: Build financial models: revenue projections, unit economics, and discounted cash flow analyses. Use when the user asks for forecasts, scenarios, or valuation math.

name: terraform-review
description: Review Terraform plans and modules for destructive changes, drift, and security misconfigurations. Use when the user wants infrastructure-as-code changes checked before apply.

name: accessibility-audit
description: Audit web pages and flows against WCAG criteria: contrast, keyboard navigation, ARIA usage, and screen-reader behavior. Use when the user asks how accessible their UI is.

name: changelog-writer
description: Draft release notes and changelogs from commit history and merged pull requests, grouped by impact. Use when the user is cutting a release and needs the changes summarized.

name: slide-deck-builder
description: Turn briefs, docs, or data into presentation outlines and slide content with a clear narrative arc. Use when the user needs a pitch, review, or teaching deck drafted.

name: database-migration
description: Plan and write schema migrations: column splits, backfills, and zero-downtime rollout strategies. Use when the user needs to change a live database schema safely.

name: incident-postmortem
description: Draft blameless incident postmortems: timeline reconstruction, contributing factors, and follow-up actions. Use when the user needs an outage written up after the fact.
